items.title: Tabular supervised learning
items.description: Gradient boosting is a default strong baseline for structured data—customer churn, fraud, pricing, and ranking features—where deep nets often add little over a tuned GBM.

items.title: Learning-to-rank and recommender features
items.description: Search and ads systems stack hundreds of engineered features; boosted trees capture non-linear feature interactions for relevance and CTR models.

items.title: Insurance and credit scoring
items.description: Regulators and analysts favor interpretable tree ensembles; staged SHAP or partial dependence on boosted trees explain segment-level risk drivers.

items.title: Competitive ML benchmarks (Kaggle-style)
items.description: Many winning tabular solutions use XGBoost/LightGBM variants of gradient boosting with careful hyperparameter tuning and feature engineering.
